turns.user_message: Honestly, doc, I don't know why I'm here. I don't think I need to quit smoking. It's not a big deal. I've been smoking for 15 years, and I'm fine.
step2_rag_chunks: If someone offers you a cigarette, practice politely saying no and reminding yourself why you decided to quit.

Quitting smoking reduces the risk of lung disease, heart disease, and many types of cancer.

step4_session_phase.phase_instruction: You are in the EXPLORATION phase. You know the basics. Now gently explore what situations or emotions make them smoke more — triggers like stress, meals, social settings, boredom. Do NOT suggest any strategies yet. Just understand their situation.

Therapist: Hello, how are you? Welcome to EDosthi. I'm here to help you with tobacco cessation. Would you like to tell me about your tobacco use?
Patient: Honestly, doc, I don't know why I'm here. I don't think I need to quit smoking. It's not a big deal. I've been smoking for 15 years, and I'm fine.

Therapist: I understand that you might not think it's a big deal, but as your healthcare provider, I want to make sure you're aware of the risks associated with smoking. Can you tell me what you think about the idea of quitting?

Patient: Quitting? Nah, I don't think so. I've tried it before, and it didn't work out. I don't want to gain weight, and I don't want to give up the social aspect of smoking. It's part of who I am, you know?
Current patient message:
Quitting? Nah, I don't think so. I've tried it before, and it didn't work out. I don't want to gain weight, and I don't want to give up the social aspect of smoking. It's part of who I am, you know?
Therapist:
step7_response: It sounds like the social aspect of smoking is really important to you, and that's part of why you're hesitant to quit. Can you tell me more about what happens when you're with friends or in social situations and everyone is smoking?

turns.user_message: I don't know...I've seen friends who quit and they just ballooned up. I don't want that to happen to me. And what about when I'm out with friends at the bar? Smoking is just part of the experience.
step2_rag_chunks: If someone offers you a cigarette, practice politely saying no and reminding yourself why you decided to quit.

Therapist: Hello, how are you? Welcome to EDosthi. I'm here to help you with tobacco cessation. Would you like to tell me about your tobacco use?
Patient: Honestly, doc, I don't know why I'm here. I don't think I need to quit smoking. It's not a big deal. I've been smoking for 15 years, and I'm fine.

Therapist: I understand that you might not think it's a big deal, but as your healthcare provider, I want to make sure you're aware of the risks associated with smoking. Can you tell me what you think about the idea of quitting?

Patient: Quitting? Nah, I don't think so. I've tried it before, and it didn't work out. I don't want to gain weight, and I don't want to give up the social aspect of smoking. It's part of who I am, you know?

Therapist: I understand that quitting can be tough, especially when it's tied to your identity and social life. But what if I told you that there are ways to manage weight gain and still quit smoking? Would you be open to exploring those options?

Patient: I don't know...I've seen friends who quit and they just ballooned up. I don't want that to happen to me. And what about when I'm out with friends at the bar? Smoking is just part of the experience.

I don't know...I've seen friends who quit and they just ballooned up. I don't want that to happen to me. And what about when I'm out with friends at the bar? Smoking is just part of the experience.
Therapist:
step7_response: You're worried about gaining weight and feeling left out when you're with friends at the bar. Would you be willing to try politely saying no to cigarettes when offered, and remind yourself why you decided to quit, just to see how it feels?
